author: takezoh
description: Sync your vault with cloud storage (Google Drive, OneDrive, Dropbox). - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: Takehito Gondo
manifest.description: Sync your vault with cloud storage (Google Drive, OneDrive, Dropbox).
manifest.name: Air Sync

name: Air Sync

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Fixed

- Config sync no longer misses remote-only files (e.g. under `.obsidian/`) until a manual Rescan — enabling config sync, or changing its dot-path or ignore settings, now triggers the needed catch-up automatically.
- The "Dot-prefixed paths to sync" setting no longer silently drops nested entries like `foo/.bar`.

## Changed

- Config sync is no longer marked experimental.
- Settings now clarify that config sync changes aren't applied instantly — they ride along on the next sync run, and require reloading the affected plugin (or restarting Obsidian) to take effect.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Added

- **Config sync (experimental)**: Sync Obsidian's own settings across devices.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Fixed

- Improved typed-lint compatibility across different ESLint and TypeScript toolchains.
- Reduced false-positive `no-unsafe-*` and type-assertion warnings in header handling, hashing, error parsing, protocol callback params, and backend helpers.
- Added a reproducible `lint:bot-repro` workflow to validate the same typed-lint-sensitive files in an isolated toolchain environment.

## Changed

- Updated wording around config directory sync support.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Fixed

- Fixed Google Drive large-file uploads on runtimes that expose the resumable upload `Location` response header with different casing, including Android-style `Location` headers.
- Improved diagnostics when Google Drive starts a resumable upload but does not return an upload URL.
- Reduced repeated retry loops for the same permanent local-origin sync failure after the required cold recovery pass, while keeping transient and rate-limit failures retryable immediately after recovery.

## Changed

- Sync failure quarantine now uses stable internal failure codes instead of human-readable error messages, so diagnostic wording and header ordering do not affect recovery behavior.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Added
- **OneDrive (custom app)** and **Dropbox (custom app)** backends — bring your own public PKCE client id (Entra application ID / Dropbox app key) instead of using the built-in connection. For OneDrive the custom backend also lets you pick the account type (personal / common / work & school / specific tenant GUID), which is what enables work or school (Azure AD) accounts that the built-in OneDrive connection cannot reach.

### Changed
- The built-in OneDrive backend now explicitly notes in its settings that it supports **personal Microsoft accounts only**, and points users with work / school accounts at the new OneDrive (custom app) backend.
- The backend selector in settings now groups built-in backends (Google Drive, OneDrive, Dropbox) above the custom-app variants.

### Fixed
- OneDrive (custom app): an empty custom tenant value now fails closed (the backend reports as unauthorized) instead of silently falling back to the personal tenant. Previously only the settings UI guarded this; a non-UI auth path could authorize against the wrong tenant.

### Docs
- Corrected stale "REPLACE_ME" placeholder notes — the shipped OneDrive and Dropbox client ids are the real committed public identifiers, not placeholders.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Added

- **Smarter large-file throttling during sync.** The transfer pool now tracks how many bytes are in-flight, not just how many files. Small text files run at full concurrency; large attachments self-throttle so peak memory stays predictable on both mobile and desktop.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Fixed

- **Mobile cold start no longer runs a redundant second sync.** Opening the app from a cold start used to fire a duplicate full scan right after startup — a brief "Syncing…" flash followed by a second "Everything up to date" notice. Air Sync now re-syncs on returning to the foreground only after the app has actually left it, so the wasteful startup re-scan is gone.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Added

- **Adaptive transfer concurrency.** Syncs now scale how many files they transfer in parallel based on observed throughput, and back off and retry per action when the backend pushes back — faster syncs on good connections, steadier behavior on flaky ones.
- **Adaptive full-scan listing for Google Drive.** Full-vault scans on Google Drive now adapt their listing concurrency, speeding up scans of large vaults.

## Fixed

- **Dropbox folder renames no longer re-download the whole folder.** Renaming a remote folder in Dropbox is now detected as a rename instead of re-pulling every file one by one.
- **Steadier behavior under bursty load.** Hardened the adaptive concurrency pool's burst response and full-scan drain, and fixed several edge cases in the sync plan executor and its retry path.

## Changed

- Removed the "Preview" label from the Dropbox and OneDrive options in settings and reordered the backend list.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Fixed

- **Duplicate "Everything up to date" notice on mobile.** Returning to the app on mobile could fire several sync triggers back-to-back, showing the "Everything up to date" notice twice. The plugin now shows a single notice per resume.
- **Edits made while a sync was already running could be slow to sync.** A note edited or renamed during an in-progress sync cycle could drop off the fast change-tracking path and wait for the next full vault scan to pick it up — slower than necessary on large vaults edited continuously. Such edits now stay on the fast path and sync on the next cycle. (No edits were ever lost, only delayed.)

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Added

- **Sync with OneDrive (Preview).** Air Sync can now sync your vault into a folder
  inside OneDrive's app folder, alongside Google Drive and Dropbox. Sign in with
  your Microsoft account and pick the destination folder from an in-app list.

## Changed

- **Dropbox sign-in is now fully in-app.** Authorization returns straight to Obsidian
  instead of bouncing through a browser relay page, and you choose the destination
  folder from an in-app modal that lists the folders in Dropbox's app folder — no
  browser round-trip required.

## Fixed

- **Smaller local storage footprint.** The base copy used for three-way conflict
  merges is now compressed in the local database, reducing on-disk usage.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Fixed

- **Mobile: iOS Safari sync recovery** — On iOS, Safari closes IndexedDB connections when the app is backgrounded or under memory pressure. The stale connection handle was causing every subsequent sync transaction to fail with "The database connection is closing", leaving sync broken until the app was force-quit. Air Sync now detects this error and automatically reopens the connection, so sync self-heals on the next cycle without a task-kill.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Added
- **Sync with Dropbox (Preview).** Sync your vault into a folder inside Dropbox's app folder, with a picker to choose which one.
- **Choose which Google Drive folder to sync into.** Pick an existing Drive folder during setup; Air Sync builds a convention-based remote vault inside it instead of assuming a fixed location.
- **Separate "sync complete" notifications from logging** so you can keep completion notices without the verbose log, or vice versa.
- **Conflict resolution history** — resolved conflicts are now recorded for later review.

### Fixed
- Exclude OS junk files (`.DS_Store`, `desktop.ini`, `thumbs.db`) from sync on every backend.
- Crash-safe deletes: the remote cache is now persisted at checkpoint commit so an interrupted sync can't lose deletions.
- Google Drive: persist a rotated refresh token, cap pagination so a misbehaving server can't loop forever, and harden delta handling against stale writes.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Added
- **Sync with Dropbox (Preview).** Sync your vault into a folder inside Dropbox's app folder, with a picker to choose which one. While in Preview, you enter your own Dropbox app key in settings (Air Sync's own key isn't built in yet).
- **Choose which Google Drive folder to sync into.** Pick an existing Drive folder during setup; Air Sync builds a convention-based remote vault inside it instead of assuming a fixed location.
- **Separate "sync complete" notifications from logging** so you can keep completion notices without the verbose log, or vice versa.
- **Conflict resolution history** — resolved conflicts are now recorded for later review.

### Fixed

- Google Drive: persist a rotated refresh token, cap pagination so a misbehaving server can't loop forever, and harden delta handling against stale writes.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
This release moves the manual sync control to the status bar and keeps your screen awake during sync on mobile.

## Added

- **Your screen stays awake during sync on mobile.** Air Sync now holds a screen wake lock while a sync is running, so a large or slow sync finishes instead of stalling when the screen would otherwise turn off.

## Changed

- **Manual sync moved to the status bar.** "Sync now" and the live sync status now sit together in the status bar instead of the ribbon. The `Air Sync: Sync now` command is unchanged.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
This release focuses on sync reliability — hidden files, interrupted syncs, and protecting your notes from wrong deletions.

## Fixed

- **Hidden / config files now sync reliably.** Dot-prefixed paths (`.obsidian`, `.airsync`, and nested folders like `foo/.bar`) are excluded from Obsidian's in-memory index, which made them get re-detected every cycle and never converge. They're now read and written through the raw adapter, so they sync to completion. (A hidden path still only syncs when you enable *Sync dot paths* and it isn't ignored.)
- **Interrupted syncs recover instead of getting stuck on "Synced".** Quitting mid-sync — or a cycle with a failed action — could leave the vault half-synced while the status still read *Synced*. The delta cursor is now committed only after a fully successful cycle, and the next run forces a full reconcile to rediscover anything left behind.
- **Folder renames no longer trigger spurious deletes.** Renaming a folder on the remote could strand the moved files and resurrect them on a later sync. Renames now rewrite baselines to the new path; if the destination is already occupied, the bulk rename is skipped and files converge one by one.
- **Erroneous deletions are now prevented structurally.** The old volume-based safety check — which could silently drop a legitimate single-file deletion — is replaced by authoritative local-absence checks, gated on the workspace being fully loaded, so a file missing only from a half-loaded index is never deleted. Genuine deletions still propagate.

## Added

- **"Rescan vault" action** in settings to force a full reconcile on demand.

## Internal

- Hardened test harness (decision-table suite, convergence/conflict tests, coverage gates) and lint guards enforcing the architecture's design principles.
